brief_justification: Correctly identifies Law of Large Numbers with valid reasoning mapped precisely to all three systems; goes three levels deep — LLN mechanism, variance scaling (σ²/N), and CLT as convergence characterizer; opens with an explicit critical check distinguishing aggregate from emergent before proceeding; builds and tests an additive model for each system as a concrete verification step; acknowledges gas molecules do interact but qualifies why short-range collisions don't break the additive model assumption; explicitly considers emergence as an alternative and rejects it with a structured falsification test; every conclusion has a derivation trail including a table and the 1/√N fluctuation derivation; directly targets the negative gate (isolated bird analysis explains swarm) by proving the three systems are aggregate-not-emergent, explaining why the anti-pattern doesn't apply; zero evidence of isolated actor analysis or interaction-effect blindness — suppressed modes are explicitly named and avoided; follows the topology from agent identification through the M-node additive-model check to the G1 gate, correctly routing to DISCARD:merely_aggregate and exiting with LLN.
